Ray is an AI compute engine. Ray consists of a core distributed runtime and a set of AI Libraries for accelerating ML workloads.

comment.body:
### Incompatible mistune version pinned for nbconvert 7.x **High Severity** Bumping `nbconvert` to 7.17.1 without updating `mistune` from 0.8.4 to >=2.0.3 will cause runtime failures. `nbconvert` 7.x requires `mistune>=2.0.3,<4` because mistune 2.0 was a complete API rewrite (e.g., `BlockGrammar` was removed). The pinned `mistune==0.8.4` (marked `# via nbconvert`) is incompatible with this new version, and any code path that triggers nbconvert's markdown processing will crash with an `AttributeError`.
Additional Locations (1) - [`python/requirements_compiled_py3.13.txt#L1200-L1201`](https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/6d2bb0fc8e8544a028c9201bf5d844e1c59dc87a/python/requirements_compiled_py3.13.txt#L1200-L1201)
 
Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit 6d2bb0fc8e8544a028c9201bf5d844e1c59dc87a. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).

payload.description: Ray is an AI compute engine. Ray consists of a core distributed runtime and a set of AI Libraries for accelerating ML workloads.

comment.body:
If this is a local object, try to pin it right away when active (in the pull stage)：https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/8b057452ea8975852174f452181aab4ab601bf99/src/ray/object_manager/pull_manager.cc#L167
If not, we'll try to pin it once the object arrives: https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/8b057452ea8975852174f452181aab4ab601bf99/src/ray/object_manager/pull_manager.cc#L586

And we don't need to worry about pin failure. There is indeed a race condition where the raylet thinks an object is local and the object store just evicted it. But this is the same as master. the master branch and this PR will both get corrected, as there is a tick that tries to make all active bundles local again. This PR does not change that behavior.

issue.title: [Core][UV] uv argument parsing is wrong

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Something that is supposed to be working; but isn't

labels.name: P1
labels.description: Issue that should be fixed within a few weeks

labels.name: core
labels.description: Issues that should be addressed in Ray Core

issue.body:
### What happened + What you expected to happen

I actually don't understand how people use uv with ray, because on the latest versions it's just broken for me

When running without "uv run", it defaults to my local uv binary: 
```
bash: line 1: /home/leviska/.pyenv/versions/venv-3.12.5/bin/uv: No such file or directory
```
Which obviously does not exist on cluster

When running with `"py_executable": "uv run"`, it then fails to go through convoluted argument parsing 
```
# uv_runtime_env_hook.py:~370

    # Calculate how many arguments were consumed by the parser.
    # Since disable_interspersed_args() is set, parsing stops at the first
    # unrecognized argument (the command), so all consumed args are uv options.
    args_consumed = original_length - len(command) - 1
    uv_run_args = cmdline[: 2 + args_consumed]
    logger.error("UV CUT %s %s %s", cmdline, original_length, command)
```

Prints
```
['uv', 'run', '-m', 'ray.util.client.server', '--address=172.18.0.2:6379', '--host=127.0.0.1', '--port=23007', '--mode=specific-server'] 
6 
['--address', '172.18.0.2:6379', '--host=127.0.0.1', '--port=23007', '--mode=specific-server']
```
And if you're observant enough, you will notice that original `cmdline` has `--address=172.18.0.2:6379` as a single argument, and the parsed `command` has `'--address', '172.18.0.2:6379'` as two separate arguments, and this mismatch makes cutting wrong, so instead of getting `['uv', 'run', '-m', 'ray.util.client.server']` we get `['uv', 'run', '-m']` and the next parser fails with very non obvious error
```
usage: __main__.py [-h] [--directory DIRECTORY] [-m MODULE]
__main__.py: error: argument -m/--module: expected one argument
```

The reason argument is sliced is due to 

```python
# uv_runtime_env_hook.py:~250

    try:
        parser._process_args(parser.largs, parser.rargs, options)
    except optparse.BadOptionError as err:
        # If we hit an argument that is not recognized, we put it
        # back into the unconsumed arguments
        logger.error("UV FFF %s %s", err.opt_str, parser.rargs)
        # this logic
        parser.rargs = [err.opt_str] + parser.rargs
    return options, parser.rargs
```

### Versions / Dependencies

PYTHON_VERSION=3.12.9
RAY_VERSION=2.55.0
ubuntu:24.04

### Reproduction script

I've tested this with this docker
```
############################################
# Base image: Ubuntu 24.04
############################################
FROM ubuntu:24.04

ARG PYTHON_VERSION=3.12.9
ARG RAY_VERSION=2.55.0

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    LANG=C.UTF-8 \
    LC_ALL=C.UTF-8 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    PATH=/app/.venv/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH \
    VIRTUAL_ENV=/app/.venv \
    UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=/app/.venv

############################################
# System dependencies
############################################
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    wget \
    unzip \
    procps \
    iproute2 \
    git \
    gdb \
    rsync \
    libnuma1 \
    \
    libssl3 \
    libffi8 \
    libbz2-1.0 \
    liblzma5 \
    libsqlite3-0 \
    libncursesw6 \
    libuuid1 \
    libfreetype6 \
    libpng16-16 \
 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
 && update-ca-certificates

############################################
# uv
############################################
RUN curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | env INSTALLER_NO_MODIFY_PATH=1 sh \
 && mv /root/.local/bin/uv /usr/local/bin/uv \
 && mv /root/.local/bin/uvx /usr/local/bin/uvx

############################################
# Python + Ray (via pyproject.toml)
############################################
COPY pyproject.toml /app/pyproject.toml
RUN cd /app && uv python install ${PYTHON_VERSION} && uv sync

############################################
# Verify installations
############################################
RUN python --version && ray --version && uv --version

WORKDIR /app

EXPOSE 6379 8265 10001

CMD ["ray", "start", "--head", "--block", "--dashboard-host=0.0.0.0"]
```
this pyproject
```
[project]
name = "cluster-test"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Add your description here"
requires-python = "==3.12.9"
dependencies = [
    "ray[default]==2.55.0",
]
```
and this docker compose
```
services:
  ray-head:
    build: .
    shm_size: "12gb"
#    volumes:
#      - ./ray/python/ray:/app/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/ray # to override ray with local prints
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
      - "8265:8265"
      - "10001:10001"
    command:
      [
        "ray",
        "start",
        "--head",
        "--block",
        "--dashboard-host=0.0.0.0",
        "--num-cpus=4",
        "--memory=68719476736",
        "--verbose",
      ]
    environment:
      - RAY_BACKEND_LOG_LEVEL=debug
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "4"
          memory: 64G

  ray-worker:
    build: .
    shm_size: "12gb"
#    volumes:
#      - ./ray/python/ray:/app/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/ray
    command:
      [
        "ray",
        "start",
        "--address=ray-head:6379",
        "--block",
        "--num-cpus=8",
        "--memory=137438953472",
        "--verbose",
      ]
    environment:
      - RAY_BACKEND_LOG_LEVEL=debug
    depends_on:
      - ray-head
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "8"
          memory: 128G
```

### Issue Severity

None

issue.title: [data][llm] `SGLangEngineProcessor`: support `trust_remote_code` models in telemetry config loading

labels.name: good-first-issue
labels.description: Great starter issue for someone just starting to contribute to Ray

labels.name: contribution-welcome
labels.description: Community contributions for this issue will be welcomed and prioritized for review.

issue.body:
### Description                                                                                                                    
Models with `trust_remote_code=True` (e.g. MiniMax-M2.1) bundle custom Python config files. In `sglang_engine_proc.py`, the telemetry call:

```
hf_config = transformers.AutoConfig.from_pretrained(config.model_source)
```

1. Doesn't pass `trust_remote_code`
2. Doesn't download model files first (so .py configs aren't available locally)
3. Has no error handling — a failure here crashes the entire processor build
                                                                                                                             
The vLLM processor already handles all three. ray-project/ray#60344 further improves the vLLM path's download mode selection but does not address SGLang.


### Completion Criteria                                             
- Call `download_model_files()` before `AutoConfig.from_pretrained()`, using `EXCLUDE_SAFETENSORS` mode when `trust_remote_code=True` and `TOKENIZER_ONLY` otherwise
- Pass `trust_remote_code` from `config.engine_kwargs` to `AutoConfig.from_pretrained()`                                         
- Wrap in `try/except` so telemetry failures fall back to `DEFAULT_MODEL_ARCHITECTURE` instead of crashing                     

### Reference
https://github.com/ray-project/ray/pull/60344#discussion_r2985231645

### Use case

_No response_

issue.title: core(uv): Fix uv existence check in UVProcessor

labels.name: core
labels.description: Issues that should be addressed in Ray Core

labels.name: go
labels.description: add ONLY when ready to merge, run all tests

labels.name: community-contribution
labels.description: Contributed by the community

issue.body:
## Description
Recent versions of uv require a `pyproject.toml` to exist in the current directory for the command below to work:
```bash
python -m uv version
```

This command is currently used in the uv existence check performed by the UVProcessor. The uv existence check fails even if uv is installed, because the command returns with an exception due to the missing `pyproject.toml`.
When ray is installed in a python environment that contains pip this falls back to ray trying to "reinstall uv",  while it makes the creation of any environment fail completely in those cases where pip is not installed (i.e., uv native python environments).

To guarantee the uv existence check works in all cases, regardless of the presence of a pyproject, one should use the command shown below:

```bash
python -m uv self version
```

This PR fixes the uv existence check in the UVProcessor, by updating the command used for performing the check according to the above description.

## Related issues
Fixes #60570 

## Additional information

comment.body: @christian-pinto is the command you specified supported across all `uv` versions?

issue.title: [Core] Ray dependency on Pydantic is too weak on Python 3.14

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Something that is supposed to be working; but isn't

labels.name: triage
labels.description: Needs triage (eg: priority, bug/not-bug, and owning component)

issue.body:
### What happened + What you expected to happen

1. Installing Ray on Python 3.14 with Pydantic 2.12.5
2. Start Ray (`ray start --head`)
3. Ray fails with a Pydantic stack trace about incompatibility with 3.14

The expected behavior is for Ray to start.

Upgrading Pydantic to 2.13.1 fixes the problem, indicating that Ray's dependency specification is too loose. Tightening the lower bound on acceptable Pydantic versions in the Ray distribution will reduce the likelihood of users accidentally installing incompatible combinations.

This is most likely to hit users upgrading Ray in an existing project, but not upgrading all dependencies along with it. Tightening the bound will tell tools like `uv` that `pydantic` also needs to be upgraded when upgrading to the newer `ray`.

### Versions / Dependencies

Python: 3.14.0
MacOS (latest)
Ray: 2.55
Pydantic: 2.12.5

### Reproduction script

Minimal `uv` invocation to demonstrate the problem:

```console
$ uvx -p 3.14 --with 'pydantic==2.12.*' ray start --head
/Users/mde48/.cache/uv/archive-v0/xuxDjTOd3heaxMnFnq9lD/lib/python3.14/site-packages/ray/_common/usage/usage_lib.py:660: SyntaxWarning: 'return' in a 'finally' block
  return result
/Users/mde48/.cache/uv/archive-v0/xuxDjTOd3heaxMnFnq9lD/lib/python3.14/site-packages/ray/_common/pydantic_compat.py:65: UserWarning: Core Pydantic V1 functionality isn't compatible with Python 3.14 or greater.
  from pydantic.v1 import (
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/mde48/.cache/uv/archive-v0/xuxDjTOd3heaxMnFnq9lD/bin/ray", line 6, in <module>
    from ray.scripts.scripts import main
  File "/Users/mde48/.cache/uv/archive-v0/xuxDjTOd3heaxMnFnq9lD/lib/python3.14/site-packages/ray/scripts/scripts.py", line 2860, in <module>
    from ray.util.state.state_cli import (
    ...<4 lines>...
    )
  File "/Users/mde48/.cache/uv/archive-v0/xuxDjTOd3heaxMnFnq9lD/lib/python3.14/site-packages/ray/util/state/__init__.py", line 1, in <module>
    from ray.util.state.api import (
    ...<22 lines>...
    )
  File "/Users/mde48/.cache/uv/archive-v0/xuxDjTOd3heaxMnFnq9lD/lib/python3.14/site-packages/ray/util/state/api.py", line 21, in <module>
    from ray.util.state.common import (
    ...<20 lines>...
    )
  File "/Users/mde48/.cache/uv/archive-v0/xuxDjTOd3heaxMnFnq9lD/lib/python3.14/site-packages/ray/util/state/common.py", line 32, in <module>
    from ray.dashboard.modules.job.pydantic_models import JobDetails
  File "/Users/mde48/.cache/uv/archive-v0/xuxDjTOd3heaxMnFnq9lD/lib/python3.14/site-packages/ray/dashboard/modules/job/pydantic_models.py", line 12, in <module>
    class DriverInfo(BaseModel):
    ...<8 lines>...
        )
  File "/Users/mde48/.cache/uv/archive-v0/xuxDjTOd3heaxMnFnq9lD/lib/python3.14/site-packages/pydantic/v1/main.py", line 221, in __new__
    inferred = ModelField.infer(
        name=var_name,
    ...<3 lines>...
        config=config,
    )
  File "/Users/mde48/.cache/uv/archive-v0/xuxDjTOd3heaxMnFnq9lD/lib/python3.14/site-packages/pydantic/v1/fields.py", line 504, in infer
    return cls(
        name=name,
    ...<7 lines>...
        field_info=field_info,
    )
  File "/Users/mde48/.cache/uv/archive-v0/xuxDjTOd3heaxMnFnq9lD/lib/python3.14/site-packages/pydantic/v1/fields.py", line 434, in __init__
    self.prepare()
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~^^
  File "/Users/mde48/.cache/uv/archive-v0/xuxDjTOd3heaxMnFnq9lD/lib/python3.14/site-packages/pydantic/v1/fields.py", line 544, in prepare
    self._set_default_and_type()
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^
  File "/Users/mde48/.cache/uv/archive-v0/xuxDjTOd3heaxMnFnq9lD/lib/python3.14/site-packages/pydantic/v1/fields.py", line 576, in _set_default_and_type
    raise errors_.ConfigError(f'unable to infer type for attribute "{self.name}"')
pydantic.v1.errors.ConfigError: unable to infer type for attribute "id"
```

Working version:

```console
$ uvx -p 3.14 --with 'pydantic==2.13.*' ray start --head
/Users/mde48/.cache/uv/archive-v0/9HSHQgyZ8oP_P__EoYwVY/lib/python3.14/site-packages/ray/_common/usage/usage_lib.py:660: SyntaxWarning: 'return' in a 'finally' block
  return result
Usage stats collection is disabled.

Local node IP: 127.0.0.1

--------------------
Ray runtime started.
--------------------

Next steps

  To connect to this Ray cluster:
    import ray
    ray.init()

  To terminate the Ray runtime, run
    ray stop

  To view the status of the cluster, use
    ray status
```

### Issue Severity

Low: It annoys or frustrates me.

comment.body: Hi @aslonnie, could you take a look? thanks.

issue.title: [Bug] experimental_mutable_object_manager.cc -  Check failed: RAY_PREDICT_TRUE(_left_ != _right_)  0 vs 0

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Something that is supposed to be working; but isn't

labels.name: P1
labels.description: Issue that should be fixed within a few weeks

labels.name: core
labels.description: Issues that should be addressed in Ray Core

labels.name: contribution-welcome
labels.description: Community contributions for this issue will be welcomed and prioritized for review.

issue.body:
### What happened + What you expected to happen

## Hardware

2x DGX Sparks

## Software

See Reproduction script

## Error

The application starts successfully, when using `curl` to communicate to the app, the following error appears.

```
...
(APIServer pid=1) INFO:     Application startup complete.
(EngineCore pid=107) INFO 04-03 12:10:43 [ray_executor.py:561] RAY_CGRAPH_get_timeout is set to 300
(EngineCore pid=107) INFO 04-03 12:10:43 [ray_executor.py:565] VLLM_USE_RAY_COMPILED_DAG_CHANNEL_TYPE = nccl

Please report this issue at https://github.com/ray-project/ray/issues and we'll work with you to fix it. Check failed: RAY_PREDICT_TRUE(_left_ != _right_)  0 vs 0
*** StackTrace Information ***

/usr/local/lib/python3.12/dist-packages/ray/_raylet.so(_ZN3ray12experimental20MutableObjectManager15RegisterChannelERKNS_8ObjectIDESt10unique_ptrIN6plasma13MutableObjectESt14default_deleteIS7_EEb+0xb4) [0xfb0782d7d3f4] ray::experimental::MutableObjectManager::RegisterChannel()
/usr/local/lib/python3.12/dist-packages/ray/_raylet.so(_ZN3ray4core12experimental21MutableObjectProvider21RegisterReaderChannelERKNS_8ObjectIDE+0x78) [0xfb0782d71a48] ray::core::experimental::MutableObjectProvider::RegisterReaderChannel()
/usr/local/lib/python3.12/dist-packages/ray/_raylet.so(_ZN3ray4core10CoreWorker39ExperimentalRegisterMutableObjectReaderERKNS_8ObjectIDE+0x20) [0xfb0782d006a0] ray::core::CoreWorker::ExperimentalRegisterMutableObjectReader()
/usr/local/lib/python3.12/dist-packages/ray/_raylet.so(+0x8d7000) [0xfb0782c47000] __pyx_pw_3ray_7_raylet_10CoreWorker_49experimental_channel_register_reader()
VLLM::EngineCore(PyObject_Vectorcall+0x4c) [0x4ce52c] PyObject_Vectorcall
VLLM::EngineCore(_PyEval_EvalFrameDefault+0x67c) [0x4b2fdc] _PyEval_EvalFrameDefault
VLLM::EngineCore(PyEval_EvalCode+0x84) [0x5a0f44] PyEval_EvalCode
VLLM::EngineCore() [0x5f0c0c]
VLLM::EngineCore() [0x5e97e8]
VLLM::EngineCore(PyRun_StringFlags+0x70) [0x5e3830] PyRun_StringFlags
VLLM::EngineCore(PyRun_SimpleStringFlags+0x40) [0x5e3530] PyRun_SimpleStringFlags
VLLM::EngineCore(Py_RunMain+0x404) [0x5df734] Py_RunMain
VLLM::EngineCore(Py_BytesMain+0x24) [0x5856f4] Py_BytesMain
/usr/lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6(+0x27400) [0xfb0948527400]
/usr/lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6(__libc_start_main+0x98) [0xfb09485274d8] __libc_start_main
VLLM::EngineCore(_start+0x30) [0x585570] _start

(APIServer pid=1) ERROR 04-03 12:10:44 [core_client.py:667] Engine core proc EngineCore died unexpectedly, shutting down client.
(APIServer pid=1) ERROR 04-03 12:10:44 [async_llm.py:707] AsyncLLM output_handler failed.
(APIServer pid=1) ERROR 04-03 12:10:44 [async_llm.py:707] Traceback (most recent call last):
(APIServer pid=1) ERROR 04-03 12:10:44 [async_llm.py:707]   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/dist-packages/vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py", line 663, in output_handler
(APIServer pid=1) ERROR 04-03 12:10:44 [async_llm.py:707]     outputs = await engine_core.get_output_async()
(APIServer pid=1) ERROR 04-03 12:10:44 [async_llm.py:707]               ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
(APIServer pid=1) ERROR 04-03 12:10:44 [async_llm.py:707]   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/dist-packages/vllm/v1/engine/core_client.py", line 985, in get_output_async
(APIServer pid=1) ERROR 04-03 12:10:44 [async_llm.py:707]     raise self._format_exception(outputs) from None
(APIServer pid=1) ERROR 04-03 12:10:44 [async_llm.py:707] vllm.v1.engine.exceptions.EngineDeadError: EngineCore encountered an issue. See stack trace (above) for the root cause.
(APIServer pid=1) INFO:     192.168.10.1:56784 - "POST /v1/chat/completions HTTP/1.1" 500 Internal Server Error
(APIServer pid=1) INFO:     Shutting down
(APIServer pid=1) INFO:     Waiting for application shutdown.
(APIServer pid=1) INFO:     Application shutdown complete.
(APIServer pid=1) INFO:     Finished server process [1]
```

## What I already tried

Differnt types of ENV variables. Also all variations of `VLLM_USE_RAY_COMPILED_DAG_CHANNEL_TYPE`. Also various docker things, but I think its not related to this issue.

### Used Images:

`vllm/vllm-openai:v0.19.0`
`vllm/vllm-openai:v0.18.1`
`vllm/vllm-openai:nightly`
`nvcr.io/nvidia/vllm:26.03-py3`
`nvcr.io/nvidia/vllm:26.02-py3`


## Disclaimer

Maybe, I miss something or I am just dumb and dont know how to deactivate the experimental_mutable_object_manager.

### Versions / Dependencies

Ray - experimental_mutable_object_manager.cc:177

### Reproduction script

Spark1:
```
docker run -dit  \
	--name ray \
	--gpus all \
	--shm-size=16GB  \
	--net=host   \
	-e GLOO_SOCKET_IFNAME=enp1s0f0np0 \
	-e NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME=enp1s0f0np0 \
	-e TP_SOCKET_IFNAME=enp1s0f0np0 \
	-e VLLM_HOST_IP=192.168.10.1 \
	-e VLLM_USE_RAY_WRAPPED_PP_COMM=0 \
	-e RAY_memory_usage_threshold=1.0 \
	-e RAY_memory_monitor_refresh_ms=0 \
	-v ./models/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface \
	-v /tmp/ray:/tmp/ray \
	--privileged \
	--entrypoint /bin/bash \
	vllm/vllm-openai:v0.19.0 -c "pip install ray cupy-cuda12x && ray start --head --block --port=6379 --node-ip-address=192.168.10.1" 
```

Spark2:
```
docker run -dit --rm  \
	--name ray \
	--gpus all \
	--shm-size=16GB  \
	--net=host   \
	-e GLOO_SOCKET_IFNAME=enp1s0f0np0 \
	-e NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME=enp1s0f0np0 \
	-e TP_SOCKET_IFNAME=enp1s0f0np0 \
	-e VLLM_HOST_IP=192.168.10.2 \
	-e VLLM_USE_RAY_WRAPPED_PP_COMM=0 \
	-e RAY_memory_usage_threshold=1.0 \
	-e RAY_memory_monitor_refresh_ms=0 \
	-v ./models/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface \
	--entrypoint /bin/bash \
	--privileged \
	vllm/vllm-openai:v0.19.0  -c "pip install ray cupy-cuda12x && ray start --block --node-ip-address=192.168.10.2 --address=192.168.10.1:6379" 
``` 

Application (Spark1):

```
docker run -it --rm  \
	--net=host --gpus all \
	-e VLLM_HOST_IP=192.168.10.1 \
	-e VLLM_USE_RAY_WRAPPED_PP_COMM=0 \
	-e VLLM_USE_RAY_COMPILED_DAG_CHANNEL_TYPE=nccl \
	-e VLLM_ENABLE_V1_MULTIPROCESSING=0 \
	-e NCCL_IB_HCA=rocep1s0f0,rocep1s0f1 \
	-e NCCL_IB_GID_INDEX=3 \
	-e NCCL_DEBUG=INFO \
	-e NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME=enp1s0f0np0 \
	-e GLOO_SOCKET_IFNAME=enp1s0f0np0 \
	-e TP_SOCKET_IFNAME=enp1s0f0np0 \
	-e RAY_ADDRESS=192.168.10.1:6379 \
	-v /tmp/ray:/tmp/ray \
	-v ./../ray/models/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface \
	--entrypoint /bin/bash  \
	vllm/vllm-openai:v0.19.0 -c "pip install ray cupy-cuda12x && python3 -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --model deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-70B --distributed-executor-backend ray --tensor-parallel-size 2 --max-model-len 8192 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.8 --trust-remote-code --enforce-eager"
```

### Issue Severity

High: It blocks me from completing my task.

issue.title: [Core][UV] Ray silently fails when it can't init pyproject

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Something that is supposed to be working; but isn't

labels.name: P1
labels.description: Issue that should be fixed within a few weeks

labels.name: core
labels.description: Issues that should be addressed in Ray Core

issue.body:
### What happened + What you expected to happen

When using uv and working dir, ray own local commands (i don't know exact commands, but something like `uv run ray/setup_worker`) can silently fail, because uv tries to run them with working_dir pyproject and it's venv, not the venv that ray server was started. 

See example for more details, but the problem is this:
1. In order for ray to produce logs, it need to run python script that will produce these logs
2. In order to run script, it seems that ray uses `uv run` internally 
3.  (!!!!)  `uv run` that ray uses for it's own scripts priorities working dir's venv/pyproject, not the venv that `ray server` was started
4. So, if pyproject is broken, the ray own task will fail to spawn with no logs

The way that this broke our setup, is that we actually had **working** pyproject with github url, and it seems that sometimes github would ratelimit uv's requiests (or some other error), and the setup will silently and **randomly** fail

I would expect, that ray should run it's internal tasks like `spawn_worker` with the same venv, that server was started. 

This also can be broken if working_dir's pyproject does not have ray as dependency, because then `uv run ray.something` will fail due to venv not having ray. Also this can break in different ways if the version is mismatched

### Versions / Dependencies

ray 2.55.0
python 3.12.9

### Reproduction script

Create an example pyproject like
```toml
[project]
name = "cluster-test"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Add your description here"
requires-python = "==3.12.9"
dependencies = [
    "ray[default]==2.55.0",
    "numpy>=2.1.0",
]
```
And run remote cluster with
```python
ray.init(
    address=args.address,
    runtime_env={
        "py_executable": "uv run",
        "working_dir": ".",
    },
)
```
This will work fine
But now replace the pyproject with something broken, like
(you might need to run with `uv run --no-sync`)
```toml
[project]
name = "cluster-test"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Add your description here"
requires-python = "==3.12.9"
dependencies = [
    "ray[default]==2.55.0",
    "numpy",
]

[tool.uv.sources]
numpy = { git = "https://github.com/this_does_not_exist/numpy.git" }
```

You would expect, that ray will fail with some error like "could not init worker: uv failed". But ray will fail with absolutely no logs. 

### Issue Severity

None

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Something that is supposed to be working; but isn't

labels.name: P1
labels.description: Issue that should be fixed within a few weeks

labels.name: core
